expectations.evidence: No customer-review-assessor agent was used. The baseline agent performed a freeform 3-perspective review without the structured agent protocol.

expectations.text: Assessment JSON output present with all three perspectives (reviewer, cso, market_expert)
expectations.evidence: Assessment uses non-standard perspective names: 'perspective_1_buyer_recognition', 'perspective_2_sales_usability', 'perspective_3_market_accuracy'. Does not match the required schema (reviewer, cso, market_expert).

expectations.text: Each perspective has 5 scored criteria with pass/warn/fail and weights
expectations.evidence: Assessment uses a single score per perspective (e.g., 8.5/10) without per-criterion breakdown, weights, or pass/warn/fail ratings. No structured criteria at all.

expectations.text: Verdict is accept, revise, or reject
expectations.evidence: No verdict field in the assessment JSON. Contains 'overall_score: 8.3' and a 'summary' string but no accept/revise/reject determination.

expectations.text: If verdict is revise, revision round executed (max 2)
expectations.evidence: No structured verdict. The agent performed 2 revision rounds but without the formal revise/accept protocol — revisions were freeform improvements, not targeted by revision_guidance.

expectations.text: Final profile retains all required fields after revision
expectations.evidence: Output profile uses a non-canonical schema: 'priorities', 'success_metrics', 'objections', 'trigger_events' instead of the required 'pain_points', 'buying_criteria', 'information_sources', 'decision_role'. Also produced 7 profiles instead of the 2-4 range.
Assessment has per-perspective round_1_improvements and round_2_findings as narrative text, not structured critical/high/optional priority tiers.

expectations.text: revision_guidance is non-empty when verdict is revise
expectations.evidence: No revision_guidance field. No verdict field. Improvements were listed as narrative text without structured guidance.

claims.evidence: Output JSON contains 7 profiles: CIO, CISO, VP Infrastructure, CFO, Head Portfolio Management & Trading, Head Grid Operations, CDO

claims.claim: Scores of 8.5, 8.0, 8.5 out of 10 across three perspectives

suggestions.reason: The baseline produces a completely different output schema (non-canonical fields, no structured scoring). This strongly validates that the skill adds value — without it, output structure is unconstrained.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline fails all 8 assertions. The skill's primary value is structural: enforcing the canonical schema, using the formal assessor agent with per-criterion scoring, and producing actionable revision guidance. The baseline output is qualitatively rich but non-standard.
